title: The Mirage of China’s Military Edge
gist: In the readable opening of this otherwise paywalled essay, Blair argues that panic over China's military edge is misguided and counterproductive, while spelling out the high stakes: a forced Chinese takeover of Taiwan would pull a nearly $1 trillion economy out of the free-market system and gravely diminish US power in East Asia.

insights.claim: A forced Chinese seizure of Taiwan would remove a nearly $1 trillion economy from the free-market system into China's state-directed, mercantilist one, and make China the dominant power in East Asia as other governments are pressured to accommodate Beijing.
insights.speaker: Dennis Blair
insights.so_what: Quantifies the economic and strategic magnitude of a Taiwan contingency, the tail risk underpinning Asia-Pacific defense and supply-chain positioning.
insights.quote: A nearly $1 trillion economy would leave the free-market system and be incorporated into China's state-directed, mercantilist one.
insights.locator: Intro / 'If China were to seize control of Taiwan by force'

insights.claim: Blair argues that panic over China's military edge is misguided and counterproductive, signaling a contrarian case against alarmism about Chinese military superiority.
insights.speaker: Dennis Blair
insights.so_what: A credible (former PACOM commander) pushback on China-threat alarmism that could temper assumptions baked into defense and risk narratives.
insights.locator: Subtitle / 'The Mirage of China's Military Edge'
